Phase D — VAR(p) per group + Granger + IRF.

For each of 10 groups, fit VAR on the 5-vector mid series (stitched, but with
a within-day modelling assumption — we just dummy-out the day boundary).
- BIC chooses p (max 10).
- Granger causality matrix per group.
- IRFs at horizon 50

2. 50-product PCA last-component bask

- ADF p, OU half-life on residual.
- Walk-forward z-score reversion strategy if ADF<0.05 and HL∈[5,1000].

Data loaders for ROUND_5.

Locates `prices_round_5_day_{day}.csv` and `trades_round_5_day_{day}.csv`,
parses with pandas, and caches a parquet copy under `data_views/` so subsequent
calls are cheap. The CSV files use `;` as the delimiter, matching the
Prosperity convention.

Publ

Schema check for ROUND_5 raw data.

Verifies that prices_round_5_day_{2,3,4}.csv and trades_round_5_day_{2,3,4}.csv
load cleanly, share the expected columns, and have plausible row counts.

Writes a machine-readable summary to stdout and updates inventory.md.
Run from this direct

For each product we evaluate every FV on day-3 (test fold A) and day-4

Round-5 product universe and group taxonomy.

Thin wrapper around the prosperity4btest CLI.

We do not implement matching ourselves — the upstream backtester
(https://github.com/nabayansaha/imc-prosperity-4-backtester, PyPI:
``prosperity4btest``) is the source of truth. This module just builds the
CLI invocation, runs it via

Statistical primitives for ROUND_5 autoresearch.

All functions accept array-likes (np.ndarray / pd.Series / list) and return
plain Python floats or small typed dicts. Statsmodels and scipy are imported
lazily so this module is cheap to import.

Public API
    adf_test(x)

Phase 0 — Data inventory.

Loads R5 prices/trades for days 2,3,4. Confirms schema, row counts, NaN map,
gap detection, tick spacing, distinct counterparties.

Outputs
    inventory.md
    sanity_checks.csv
    counterparties.csv

Phase 1 — fair-value estimator zoo.

Every FV is a CAUSAL function of the price/depth series: FV(t) depends only on
information up to and including t-1 (or t for "anchored" variants like
microprice computed from the *current* book).  Each function returns an array
the same length

Per-product distilled MR parameters.

    "mode": "TAKER" | "MM" | "IDLE",

    "sigma": float,                          # rolling std of residual on training data
    "rule": {"z_in": float, "z_out":

Phase G — intraday seasonality.

For each product, bin timestamp%10000 into 100 bins; per bin compute mean
return and vol. F-test on whether bin means differ. Build OOS overlay:
fit on day 2, test on day 3 (and 2+3 → day 4) by predicting next-tick
mean return from the per-bin cur

Phase 1 — Per-product univariate EDA.

For each of 50 R5 products: load days 2-4 stitched, run a battery of stats
(returns dist, ADF/KPSS, ACF/PACF, Hurst, VR, OU half-life, FFT, spread,
tick-grid mod-K) and dump a 6-panel PNG + a single per-product row in a
master CSV.

  mid moves

Single-file MR Trader.

Loads per-product params from `distilled_params.py`. For each product runs one
of:
  - TAKER: maintain rolling FV + residual sigma; cross-spread when |z| > z_in,
           flatten when |z| < z_out.
  - MM:    quote inside-spread (best±1) with inventory sk

Fast in-process MR simulator (taker only) — numba JIT.

Same matching semantics as the prosperity4btest engine for *aggressive*
(cross-spread) orders. Inside-spread MM is evaluated via real backtests later.

Phase 6 — Global cross-group.

- 50×50 return correlation, hierarchical cluster (linkage tree).
- 50×50 sum-stability sweep across cross-group pairs.
- Global PCA top 5 components, loadings.
- |corr| > 0.5 cross-group pairs (return space).

Phase C — Johansen higher-order cointegration.

Per group: rank, eigenvalues, cointegrating vectors. For groups with rank
≥ 2, build z-score on the SECOND vector and walk-forward test it against
forward returns of each leg.

Phase A — Sine-fit phase resolution.

For each of 7 high-sine-R² products from round 1:
- Fit `mid = A·sin(ωt+φ) + ct + d` separately on day 2, day 3, day 4.
  Compare A, ω, φ, c, d across days.  Stable across days = real cycle.
- Rolling sine fit on 3,000-tick windows.  Track ω(

- Sign-switching: lags where IC sign differs from lag-1.

ordered pair of the 50 R5 products at lags k ∈ [-200, 200].

Key design points
- We compute *standardised* cross-correlation: ρ_xy(k) = Cov(x_t, y_{t+k}) / (σ_x σ_y).

Phase 7 — HIDDEN PATTERN HUNTING.

Per product, run an aggressive battery of tests:
- Deterministic-function fits: sin(ω t)+ct+d (ω-grid), poly(t) deg 1..3.
- Lattice test: distinct prices + Markov chain entropy.
- Compressibility (gzip) ratio.
- Symbolic dynamics: discretise to
Phase E — Extended AR / lag-IC peaks

Phase 2 — threshold/rule grid search via fast in-process simulator.

For each (product, FV in top-K, rule, params):
  Walk-forward fold A: sigma fit on day-2, signal on day-3
  Walk-forward fold B: sigma fit on day-2+3, signal on day-4
  Record total_pnl day3, day4, avg daily, sh

Section 08 — Volatility regime per product/day.

ARCH-LM test on Δmid for conditional heteroskedasticity, plus rolling-stdev
summary (mean and dispersion of stdev across rolling 200-tick windows).

Section 01 — Price distributions per product/day.

Mid mean / std / skew / kurtosis / quantiles + Jarque-Bera normality test.
Section 07 — Mean-reversion half-life (OU AR(1)) per product/day.
Section 05 — Stationarity tests per product/day (ADF + KPSS).

Section 09 — Lo-MacKinlay variance ratio at q=2, 5, 10 per product/day.

Under iid increments VR(q)=1. |Z| > 1.96 rejects iid at the 5% level. VR < 1
suggests mean reversion at the q-step horizon, VR > 1 suggests momentum.

Section 03 — Tick-return statistics per product/day.

Δmid (level changes) AND log-mid increments. Both reported because some
products are near-zero (log undefined / explosive).

Section 12 — Intraday seasonality per product/day.

Bucket each day's 10,000 ticks into 10 windows of 1,000 ticks; report mean +
std of mid for each bucket per (product, day). Long-format CSV: one row per
(product, day, bucket).

Section 02 — Spread + order-book depth per product/day.

For each (product, day), measure the bid/ask spread and the typical aggregate
volume on the first three book levels.

Section 11 — Aggressor classification per trade per product/day.

R5 strips the buyer/seller fields, so we can't identify counterparties. But
each trade can still be Lee–Ready signed: compare the trade price to the
contemporaneous prevailing mid (forward-fill of the last book sna

Section 10 — Aggregate trade-flow stats per product/day.

Per (product, day): trade count, total quantity, mean / median / min / max
trade price, and ratio of trades to ticks (a proxy for flow intensity).

Driver: run every section script in order.

Each section dumps `outputs/<section>.csv` and `<section>.md`.

Section 13 — Drift / trend per product/day (linear-time fit).

Fit mid = a + b·tick. Report slope per 1,000 ticks (≈ per 100k timestamps),
intercept, R² and RMSE. Useful for picking trend-aware fair models (the C
method we used for HYDR in R4).

Section 04 — Autocorrelation of Δmid and |Δmid| per product/day.

Sign-AC(1) on Δmid is a fast diagnostic for tick-level mean reversion (negative
values) vs momentum (positive). |Δmid| AC(1) measures volatility clustering.
Section 00 — Overview: per-product row coverage, missing-data, mid availability.

Section 06 — Long-memory diagnostics (Hurst-DFA) per product/day.

H ≈ 0.5 → random walk; H < 0.5 → mean-reverting; H > 0.5 → trending.

Shared helpers for the per-product EDA scripts.

All section scripts (00_*.py … 12_*.py) import from this module so they share
data-loading, output paths, and per-product iteration.
